name: databuddy
title: Databuddy
description: Privacy-first web analytics and link shortener. View websites, track visitors, and manage short links — all from Raycast.
author: izadoesdev

preferences.name: apiKey
preferences.title: API Key
preferences.description: Your Databuddy API key. Create one at Dashboard → Organization Settings → API Keys (app.databuddy.cc).

commands.name: view-websites
commands.title: View My Websites
commands.subtitle: Databuddy
commands.description: Browse your websites with analytics for visitors, page views, referrers, and countries

commands.name: create-website
commands.title: Create Website
commands.subtitle: Databuddy
commands.description: Add a new website to your Databuddy account to start tracking analytics

commands.name: view-links
commands.title: View My Links
commands.subtitle: Databuddy
commands.description: Browse your short links with click analytics, referrers, devices, and browsers

commands.name: create-link
commands.title: Create Link
commands.subtitle: Databuddy
commands.description: Create a short link with custom slug, OG metadata, expiration, and deep linking

commands.name: view-flags
commands.title: View My Flags
commands.subtitle: Databuddy
commands.description: Browse and manage feature flags with rollout controls and status toggles

commands.name: create-flag
commands.title: Create Flag
commands.subtitle: Databuddy
commands.description: Create a new feature flag with rollout percentage and targeting rules

commands.name: view-funnels
commands.title: View My Funnels
commands.subtitle: Databuddy
commands.description: Browse funnel analytics with step-by-step conversion and drop-off rates

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
